question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request signing validation in the `overrideConfiguration()` interceptor chain to strip or replace AWS Signature V4 headers on outgoing requests, causing the S3 service to reject legitimate requests and the importer to fall back to an unauthenticated path that serves tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `URI.create(sourceProperties.getType().getEndpoint())` call with a `file://` scheme URI (if the endpoint property is misconfigured) to redirect stream file fetches to the local filesystem, where the attacker has pre-placed tampered Hashgraph record files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `isAnonymousCredentials()` check (which reads from `HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()`) by manipulating the network name property to resolve to a network that allows anonymous access, bypassing credential requirements and enabling unauthenticated access to Hashgraph stream file buckets?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `new S3StreamFileProvider(..., s3Client(source))` instantiation to cause a separate S3 client to be created per source, each with potentially different credentials, and exploit a weaker-credentialed source to upload tampered Hashgraph stream files that the importer will process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `endpointOverride` pointing to an internal network address (SSRF) to reach an internal S3-compatible service (e.g., MinIO) that the attacker has compromised, causing the importer to download tampered Hashgraph stream files from the internal service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `AwsBasicCredentials.create(credentials.getAccessKey(), credentials.getSecretKey())` call by observing credentials in plaintext application configuration files (e.g., `application.yml` exposed via Spring Boot Actuator), then using those credentials to overwrite Hashgraph stream files in the S3 bucket with tampered versions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `providers.add(provider)` accumulation in `streamFileProviders()` to cause the importer to use multiple providers simultaneously, where one provider serves legitimate files and another (attacker-controlled) serves tampered files, creating a race condition that results in tampered Hashgraph history being accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Region.of(sourceProperties.getRegion())` call with a specially crafted region string that resolves to a different S3 endpoint URL, causing the importer to connect to an attacker-controlled server and download tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ClientOverrideConfiguration` interceptor chain by injecting a malicious `ExecutionInterceptor` bean into the Spring context (via a dependency confusion or classpath manipulation attack), causing all S3 requests to be modified to fetch tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of response integrity verification in the `S3AsyncClient` configuration to accept tampered S3 responses (e.g., with modified ETag headers) without detecting that the Hashgraph stream file content has been altered?
